skill.name: ai-html-generate
skill.description: Generates semantic HTML from PDF page artifacts using a PNG image, extracted text data, and an ASCII structure preview.
skill.summary: Recreate PDF pages as semantic HTML with visual, text, and structure inputs.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the high-severity crypto, path traversal, and reconnaissance matches are false positives in Markdown instructions and examples. Real risks are limited to expected filesystem access, one local verification command, and sending document page content to the Claude API.

medium_findings.title: External AI API Receives Document Page Content
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to invoke the Claude API with a visual rendering, parsed text data, and ASCII preview. This is expected behavior, but it can disclose source document content to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly call for invoking Claude with attached page inputs. This confirms external processing, although no malicious endpoint or hidden exfiltration is present.

medium_findings.title: Local Verification Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to run a local Python verification script after HTML generation. The command is part of the documented workflow, but running project scripts from a community source requires user trust.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible and narrowly scoped, but it executes local code outside the skill file. Risk depends on the integrity of the referenced project script.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Is Core Workflow Behavior
low_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to load PDF-derived input files and save generated HTML plus metadata. This is expected for the conversion workflow and does not show unauthorized file discovery or broad filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads and writes are explicitly documented and scoped to expected page artifact paths. I did not find evidence of arbitrary path discovery or secret-file access.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command detections point to inline filenames, fenced HTML or JSON examples, and checklist text. They are not executable code in this skill file; the actual command risk is tracked separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and examples, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. No bundled script or dynamic command construction exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Relative Stylesheet Paths Are Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections point to generated HTML stylesheet links using a relative path to a styles directory. This is normal HTML output guidance, not filesystem escape logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are static HTML link examples. They do not read files, accept user paths, or concatenate untrusted path segments.

low_findings.title: Crypto And Reconnaissance Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance matches occur in ordinary prose, HTML examples, validation thresholds, or sample text. No cryptographic API, host probing, or network scanning behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found documentation text and sample HTML only. I did not find evidence of cryptography, system reconnaissance, or network scanning.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Processing Of Source Documents
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow sends visual and text representations of PDF pages to Claude. Users should avoid processing confidential documents unless their data policy allows external AI processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API call and attached inputs are central to the workflow. No hidden exfiltration was found, but external processing is confirmed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Community Workflow Runs A Local Python Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The verification step executes a local Python script. The command is documented and useful, but users should review the referenced script before running it in an untrusted repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command itself is specific and transparent. The residual risk is inherited from executing local project code.

content.user_title: Generate Semantic HTML from PDF Pages
content.value_statement: PDF page conversion often loses structure, headings, and exact page boundaries. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to build semantic HTML from image, text, and layout inputs.
actual_capabilities: Combines a rendered PNG, rich text extraction, and ASCII preview for page reconstruction.
Defines a detailed Claude prompt for valid HTML5 output with semantic elements.
Specifies required CSS class names for page, heading, list, exhibit, and navigation content.
Preserves page boundaries and requires all text to come from extracted source data.
Documents generated HTML and metadata output paths for each page.
Requires a per-page text verification command before chapter consolidation.
limitations: It is an instruction-only skill and does not include the verification script itself.
It depends on pre-existing PNG, JSON, and ASCII page artifacts from earlier workflow steps.
It sends document page content to an external Claude API workflow.
Generated HTML still requires validation because AI output can be malformed or incomplete.

use_cases.title: Convert textbook pages
use_cases.description: Recreate textbook pages as semantic HTML while preserving headings, lists, tables, and exact source text.

use_cases.title: Prepare validation-ready HTML
use_cases.description: Create page-level HTML files that can pass later text coverage and structure validation gates.

use_cases.title: Build accessible page markup
use_cases.description: Turn extracted PDF content into structured HTML with meaningful tags and reusable CSS classes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate one page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-html-generate for one prepared page. Use the PNG, rich extraction JSON, and ASCII preview to create semantic HTML.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve strict boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Avoid added or missing source text
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate HTML for this page and preserve exact page boundaries. Do not add text that is missing from the extraction data.

prompt_templates.title: Handle exhibits and lists
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-html-generate for a page with lists, figures, or tables. Apply the required semantic classes and preserve captions.

prompt_templates.title: Generate and verify workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate page HTML, save metadata, run the per-page text verification step, and stop if coverage fails the required threshold.

output_examples.input: A chapter opening page with a rendered image, extracted text spans, and an ASCII layout preview.
output_examples.output: A complete HTML page with a chapter header, section navigation, semantic headings, paragraphs, and linked stylesheet.

output_examples.input: A continuation page with body paragraphs, bullet points, and no chapter title.
output_examples.output: A semantic HTML page that starts at the extracted page boundary and preserves each bullet item in order.

output_examples.input: A page with a table or exhibit and generated metadata requirements.
output_examples.output: An HTML page with figure or table structure, captions, content metrics, input references, and generation notes.

best_practices: Review the extracted JSON and ASCII preview before generation to confirm they match the target page.
Run per-page text verification immediately after each generated HTML file is saved.
Review any page with coverage below the required threshold before continuing the workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not consolidate pages before each individual page passes text verification.
Do not allow the AI to complete broken sentences or add bridge text across page boundaries.
Do not process confidential PDFs through an external API without approval.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs a rendered PNG page image, rich extraction JSON, and an ASCII structure preview.

faq.question: Does it generate styling?
faq.answer: It links to an existing stylesheet and applies required semantic class names. It does not create the stylesheet.

faq.question: Can it process a full chapter at once?
faq.answer: The workflow is page-based. Each page should be generated and verified before consolidation.

faq.question: Why use three inputs?
faq.answer: The image provides layout, the JSON provides exact text, and the ASCII preview provides structure.

faq.question: Is the output deterministic?
faq.answer: No. The HTML is AI-generated, then checked with deterministic validation steps.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The workflow can send document content to Claude and can run a local verification script.
